_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **MONITORING** — Track the Climate Change Authority's fifth statutory review of the Carbon Credits (Carbon Farming Initiative) Act 2011 (ACCU Scheme), currently in progress. The Authority's findings will directly shape the offset-credit framework that Australian freight operators may use to manage residual emissions; monitor for public consultation windows and submit comments if the workspace purchases or sells ACCUs on behalf of clients in live events, film, or humanitarian cargo verticals. No fixed deadline has been published for completion of the fifth review; check the CCA publications page monthly. *Source: Climate Change Authority homepage, climatechangeauthority.gov.au, 2025.*

- **MONITORING** — Track the Authority's 2025 Annual Progress Report, published March 2026, which finds Australia must double the rate of emissions reductions by 2030 and triple the rate by 2035 to meet committed targets. The transport sector (road, air, domestic ocean) is explicitly modelled under the 2024 Sector Pathways Review and residual heavy-vehicle emissions are expected to remain 0–18 Mt CO₂-e in 2050. The workspace should confirm whether any current clients are Group 1 mandatory sustainability reporters (FY ending 30 June 2026 first report) who will require Scope 3 freight emissions data from the workspace. *Source: 2025 Annual Progress Report, Climate Change Authority, March 2026. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/2025-annual-progress-report*

- **WINDOW CLOSING** — Confirm Scope 3 freight emissions data supply capability before Group 1 client reporting cut-off. Group 1 large entities must file their first mandatory climate-related financial disclosures (AASB S2 / ASRS) for financial years ending 30 June 2026. Freight forwarders feeding into those supply chains must be ready to provide trip-level emissions data using National Greenhouse Accounts (NGA) factors no later than June 2026. Failure to supply verified data risks removal from preferred supplier lists. Begin data gap analysis within 30 days. *Source: Australian Sustainability Reporting Standards (ASRS) commencement, Treasury Laws Amendment (Financial Market Infrastructure and Other Measures) Act 2024, 1 January 2025. https://www.allens.com.au/insights-news/insights/2024/09/mandatory-climate-related-financial-reporting-legislation/*

- **COMPETITIVE EDGE** — Engage the CCA's Climate Policy Tracker (updated 2025; now covers 430+ policies across all jurisdictions) to identify emerging state/territory transport decarbonisation measures that may affect the workspace's road legs on Americas–Australia or intra-Australia trade lanes. Mapping regulatory exposure now enables proactive client advisory positioning ahead of competitors. *Source: Climate Policy Tracker, Climate Change Authority, 2025. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/climate-policy-tracker*
